hero.headline:
Share files directly from
your device to anywhere.
hero.subtitle: Send files of any size directly from your device without ever storing anything online.
hero.selectFolder: or <action>click here</action> to select a folder

dropzone.title: Click to browse or drag & drop files
dropzone.folderOption: or <action>click here</action> to select a folder

built_for_humans.title: Built for humans, not corporations.
built_for_humans.desc: nerdShare is free, independent, and has zero trackers. No accounts, no ads, no paywalls — just you and whoever you're sending files to. The internet the way it was meant to be.

ephemeral.title: Closes when you close.
ephemeral.desc: Your files live strictly on your device. Once a connection is established, data flows directly to your peer. Shut the tab and poof — the connection drops. No lingering uploads on third-party servers, no accidental oversharing.

e2ee.title: End-to-end encrypted.
e2ee.desc1: Using industry-standard WebRTC + DTLS routing means your data is encrypted in transit. Only your receiver can decrypt the file. We can't see your data, and we don't want to. Pinky promise. 🤙
e2ee.desc2: And because we don't host your files, we have zero infra costs for storage. Feel like sending a completely uncompressed 4K movie? Or perhaps an entire project folder? Please do. We genuinely don't mind.

cta.title: Experience the magic yourself.
cta.desc: Just drop a file into the zone, or browse to start a direct share.

landing.upload_folder_desc: {{count}} file(s) will be zipped and sent to the receiver.

nearby.title: Nearby Devices
nearby.subtitle: Looking for devices on your network…
nearby.no_devices_desc: Make sure the other device has nerdShare open and is connected to the same Wi-Fi network.
nearby.choose: Choose what you want to send

hero.title: Why nerdShare exists
hero.subtitle: It started with a simple frustration: sending a file shouldn’t require logging into five different tools.

section1.title: The moment that started it
section1.p1: One day at the office I needed to send a file to someone sitting not very far from me. Nothing unusual.
section1.p2: But the options were surprisingly annoying: upload to Teams, log in to a sharing site, wait for the upload, send a link, and wait for the download.
section1.quote: "Why does sending a file still require uploading it to some server first?"

section2.title: Discovering a simple idea
section2.p1: While searching for alternatives, I came across ToffeeShare. It immediately stood out: No accounts. No uploads to cloud storage. No waiting for a server.
section2.p2_highlight: Just open a page and send the file directly from one device to another.
section2.p3: That simplicity made me curious. Not just to use it — but to understand how it actually works.

section3.title: The curiosity spiral
section3.p1: At the time I was already working a lot with real-time technologies like WebRTC and networking systems.
section3.p2: How do the devices discover each other? How does the connection happen? How does the file move directly between peers?
section3.p3: The more I explored it, the more interesting it became. It kind of tickled my engineer brain.

section4.title: So I built nerdShare
section4.subtitle: nerdShare started as a learning experiment. I wanted to understand every piece of the system while pushing the core ideas further.

footer.title: Files should move between people, not servers.
footer.desc: nerdShare is a solo project built in spare time. Part curiosity, part engineering experiment, part attempt to make file sharing feel simpler.

hero.title: Got a question or idea?
hero.subtitle: nerdShare is built by a solo developer — feel free to reach out. I’m always listening for ways to make the web feel a little simpler.

section1.p1: nerdShare isn't a company or a big team. It's just someone who enjoys building things and exploring how real-time technologies work.

section2.title: The best way to reach out
section2.desc: Instead of a traditional contact form, the easiest way to get in touch is through <highlight>Discord</highlight>. It's faster and makes it possible to discuss tech without the usual email back-and-forth.
section2.note: * Since this is a side project, replies might not be instant, but I read everything.

section3.title: Enjoying nerdShare?
section3.desc: If you found nerdShare useful, you can support the project by buying me a coffee. It helps keep the servers running and gives me more excuses to keep building.

footer.title: Built for people, not for profiles.

hero.title: Your files stay yours.
hero.subtitle: nerdShare never stores your files. All transfers happen directly between your devices, keeping your data private and secure.

section1.title: Privacy by design
section1.desc1: nerdShare is built with a simple idea: <highlight>"The server should help devices connect — but never see the files."</highlight>
section1.desc2: Files are transferred through peer-to-peer connections and encrypted in transit using industry-standard protocols. Even the infrastructure helping devices connect cannot see the actual content of your files.

section2.title: A small reminder
section2.desc: While nerdShare focuses on privacy, security is still partly <highlight1>your responsibility</highlight1>. If you share a session link or room ID with someone, treat it like a private message. Anyone with access to that session could potentially receive the file. Keep your sharing sessions <highlight2>private and intentional</highlight2>.

section3.title: Questions?
section3.desc: nerdShare is a solo-built project, and transparency about how it works matters a lot. If you ever have questions or concerns about privacy, feel free to reach out.
section3.tag: Built by a developer who cares about your data 🤙

complete.title: Transfer Successful!
complete.desc: The file has been successfully delivered and saved on the receiver's device.

error.title: Connection Lost
error.desc: The connection was interrupted. This usually happens when a peer closes their tab or the network times out.

desc.success: Your files have been safely delivered. You can now close this tab or share another file.
desc.waiting: Keep this page open while the transfer is in progress. Closing it will terminate the connection immediately.

note.title: Sharing Note
note.desc: nerdShare is a direct device-to-device service. We don't store your files on any cloud. To ensure the transfer completes, do not close this browser tab.

waiting.title: Connected
waiting.desc: Waiting for the sender to start the transfer…

error.title: Connection Timeout
error.desc: The connection was lost due to inactivity or network issues. Please ask the sender for a new link or refresh the page.

hero.title: Receiving files <highlight>with nerdShare</highlight>
hero.desc: You are about to start a secure transfer directly from the sender. The file will be downloaded directly to your device once you accept.
